lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: [ Naitamba Islands Water Supply Development Project ]

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Partner country: Republic of Fiji / Implementing agency: KODA

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Project period: 2022-2026 (4 years) / Budget: USD 5,200,000

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Target area: Five islands in the Naitamba group, Lomaiviti Province / Beneficiaries: about 3,500 people

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Objective: Repair aging water infrastructure, replace tanks and pipelines, and strengthen village maintenance capacity

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Phase 1 target: Naitamba main island (about 1,200 people), then expand to four nearby islands

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: Status: Awaiting approval from the Ministry of National Planning (held for three months)

lines.speaker: Document
lines.text: There is a note in the remarks section.

lines.speaker: Memo
lines.text: "A similar project was attempted on the same island 10 years ago. It stopped after the officer in charge disappeared. Remember that residents still remember."
Suva, Fiji. March 2024.
KODA Fiji Office. Your predecessor returned home three days ago.
A single memo is left on the desk.

lines.speaker: Memo
lines.text: "Naitamba Island project - good luck. Sorry."
You open the project file. Last updated: six months ago.
Excuse me.
I'm looking for the KODA officer in charge.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...So the officer changed again.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: It's been six months with almost no contact.

choices.text: "I'm sorry. I only arrived a few days ago."

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...At least you're honest.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Still, I'm glad someone is here.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'm Mere, from Pacific Roots NGO.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'm here about the Naitamba Island project.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There has been no progress for six months. The island residents are tired of waiting.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...Do you know what's going on?

choices.text: "Roughly. Could we sort it out together?"
"Honestly, I'm still figuring it out. Could you brief me?"

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...Right.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'm Mere Vuniwai from Pacific Roots NGO.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: You're in charge of the Naitamba Island project, yes?

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There has been no progress for six months. How much longer are we supposed to wait?

choices.text: "I'm still assessing the situation. Could we look at it together?"
"I will contact you officially after an internal review."

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Good. Then you probably already know this -

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There are two problems. First, government approval has been stuck at the Ministry of National Planning for three months.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Second... I didn't understand this at first either.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: The island residents don't trust the project itself.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Ten years ago, they were promised the same thing. A water tank. Nothing remains.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: So even if we get approval, it means nothing without residents' trust.
Mere looks out the window for a moment.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...I think we can work this out together. For now.

choices.text: "You know the field better than I do. Could we plan the strategy together?"
"Understood. I'll start with the government side."

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Good. Remember one thing.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: This project is not paperwork. It's people. It isn't enough to make the numbers add up.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: That's exactly why it failed ten years ago. They followed the process and ignored the people.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'll go to Naitamba first. I need to understand more about Ratu Josefa.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'll contact you when I have findings. See you on the island.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: (I should schedule the Ministry of National Planning meeting first. I'll ask Wati to arrange it.)

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There are two issues.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: First, government approval. It's been stuck at the Ministry of National Planning for three months.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: The officer in charge is named Vikash, and he is hard to reach.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Second, residents' trust.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: A similar project fell apart ten years ago. People remember.
Mere starts to say more, then stops.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I think you need to meet Vikash first.

choices.text: "If the NGO network can help, please tell me."

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...Fine.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There are two problems. First, government approval has been stuck at the Ministry of National Planning for three months.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Second, the island residents don't trust this project.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There was a similar promise ten years ago. Nothing remains.
Mere pushes the file across the table.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: You have to solve both. Solving only one won't matter.

choices.text: "You're right. We need someone with field experience. Will you work with us?"
"Understood. I'll contact you once I have sorted this out."

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Ah, you're trying to set up a meeting? This is about Vikash at National Planning, right?

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: I've handled Fiji government contacts here for five years. I'll help you get appointments.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: I'll contact Vikash's office. I'll let you know when it's arranged.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Oh, and one tip -

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: In Fiji, everyone is one connection away from everyone else. Talk to people on the street too.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: You can learn a lot from locals - the mood around government, village realities, that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: You can also send progress emails to stakeholders from that computer. Small communication builds trust.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Move with the arrow keys, talk with Space. Hold Shift if you want to walk slowly.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: While the appointment is being arranged, take a look around town. Outside is Victoria Parade.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: No reply yet. Go outside and look around. I'll tell you when it's set.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Have you been to the government building? Vikash is on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: They replied. The appointment is set.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Third floor of the government building, Ministry of National Planning.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Vikash is the ODA section manager there. He handles the practical review of projects.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: Final approval goes through the minister, so he is not the final decision-maker. But if he blocks it at working level, it won't go up.

lines.speaker: Wati
lines.text: The ministry is sensitive because of a budget audit these days. Since it's the first meeting, focus on understanding the situation rather than applying pressure.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...I'll wait to hear from you.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Official contact.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Do you know how many times we've received 'official contact' already? Six.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: And nothing changed.
Mere picks up her bag.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: There are two problems: government approval and residents' trust. It's in the file.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: ...Let me know if you solve it alone.
Mere leaves.
You lost trust.
It is a work computer. The mailbox is open.

choices.text: Send a progress email to stakeholders

lines.text: You summarized the progress so far and sent an email.
Sharing even small progress is the beginning of trust.
You closed the computer.
